articles.title: Haidilao Article Changes Put Shareholder Rights And Board Powers In Focus
articles.summary: Haidilao International Holding (SEHK:6862) has proposed amendments to its articles of association. The changes are scheduled to be put to a shareholder vote at the upcoming annual general meeting (AGM). The proposals relate to the company rulebook that governs board powers, shareholder rights and corporate procedures. Haidilao operates a large hotpot restaurant chain, so any adjustment to its articles of association can matter for how decisions are made as the business evolves. For...

articles.title: Michael Burry Exposes 'Vulnerability' In Chinese Tech, Warns Of Hong Kong's 'Cayman Islands Shell' Trap
articles.summary: Famous ‘Big Short’ investor Michael Burry has issued a stark warning regarding the structural integrity of Chinese technology stocks, cautioning that most investors do not actually own the companies they believe they are betting on. The ‘Cayman Shell’ Vulnerability In a series of recent posts on X and his Substack, Burry—who famously predicted the 2008 housing market crash—detailed a critical legal flaw in the Hong Kong market. articles.title: Haidilao (SEHK:6862): Assessing Valuation as Stock Trades in a Period of Quiet Market Activity
articles.summary: When it comes to Haidilao International Holding (SEHK:6862), investors may find themselves taking a second look as the stock’s movements have started to turn some heads. There isn't a headline-grabbing event driving the latest change. Sometimes, the absence of drama is a story in itself, particularly when a company this widely followed posts a curious pattern. articles.title: Does Haidilao Offer Value After Its 30% Discount to Calculated Fair Price in 2025?
articles.summary: If you’re wondering whether now is a smart time to buy, sell, or hold Haidilao International Holding, you’re not alone. With the stock closing recently at 13.38, investors are sizing up the company’s next chapter. Even after a rocky ride in previous years, Haidilao has shown signs of resilience, notching a 12.3% return over the past year. However, it is still down 14.0% year-to-date and remains far below its five-year highs. The long-term picture might look daunting, as shares have fallen...
